mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 5 Series 530e engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned BMW 530e (B48B20B PHEV) engine typically costs £4,500–£9,500 supply only depending on generation. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 at independent specialist rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 5 Series 530e engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B48 four-cylinder engine has proven largely reliable — timing chain problems are not a documented concern. However, common B48 issues include coolant system leaks (water pump, thermostat, coolant hoses) and oil leaks from gaskets. The hybrid battery adds complexity and a potential long-term cost. Regular maintenance is essential.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 530e?

acceptedAnswer.text: B48 cooling system leaks (the most common issue), oil filter housing and valve cover gasket oil leaks, PHEV battery degradation and charging system issues, and ZF 8HP transmission issues. All are manageable with preventative maintenance and early detection.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 530e engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Against a well-maintained G30 530e (values £20,000–£35,000+), a £5,000–£6,500 reconditioned engine is 15–30% of the vehicle's value — worth it for a solid example. The BMW scrap threshold of 70% means evaluating the whole vehicle and its condition before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 530e engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code B48B20B is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The 530e badge was introduced in 2016 and offered until the G30 generation ended in 2023. Pre-LCI cars (2016–2020) use the 9.2 kWh battery; LCI cars (2020–2023) use the 12.0 kWh battery. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the B48 coolant leak, or does it require engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: B48 coolant leaks are always repairable without replacing the engine. Water pump, thermostat, and coolant hose replacement costs £400–£1,200. However, if a coolant leak leads to overheating and engine damage, more extensive work may be required. At the first sign of coolant loss or temperature fluctuations, book an inspection immediately.

problems.title: B48 Cooling System Leaks (Water Pump, Thermostat, Coolant Hoses)
problems.symptoms: Low coolant warning, coolant loss with no visible external leak, sweet smell from engine bay, temperature fluctuations, overheating.
problems.repairCost: £400–£1,200 (water pump, thermostat, or coolant hose replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (unless overheating leads to engine damage)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant loss is detected

problems.recommendation: The B48 engine has a well-documented cooling system weakness. Coolant leaks from the water pump, thermostat housing, and coolant hoses are common, with most issues appearing between 40,000 and 80,000 miles. The electric water pump often fails after 80,000 km and the thermostat housing can crack. If you notice coolant loss or temperature fluctuations, have the system pressure-tested immediately. Proactive replacement of known weak coolant hoses at 60,000 miles is recommended.

problems.title: B48 Oil Filter Housing & Valve Cover Gasket Leaks
problems.symptoms: Oil leak from the top of the engine, burning oil smell, visible oil seepage around the oil filter housing or valve cover, low oil warning light.
problems.repairCost: £300–£900 (oil filter housing gasket or valve cover gasket replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (unless oil starvation leads to engine damage)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; oil leaks can damage other components

problems.recommendation: Oil leaks from the oil filter housing and valve cover gasket are among the most common issues on B48 engines. The oil filter housing gasket can crack or leak coolant/oil around the 50,000 to 100,000-mile mark, while valve cover gasket leaks can soak spark plugs and ignition coils. Regular inspections can catch these before they become major issues. If you notice oil spots under the car or a burning smell, have it inspected promptly. Some 530e owners have reported oil mixing with coolant — a sign of a failed seal that can be expensive to repair.

problems.title: PHEV Battery Degradation & Charging System Issues
problems.symptoms: Reduced electric range, failure to charge fully, longer charging times, battery warning lights, "Unable to charge" message, the petrol engine running more often at low speeds.
problems.typicalMileage: 40,000–80,000 miles (battery degradation is age- and cycle-dependent)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if range drops significantly

problems.recommendation: BMW's hybrid batteries can lose capacity over time. Pre-LCI cars (9.2 kWh) have a WLTP electric range of around 45 km, while LCI cars (12.0 kWh) offer 61–66 km. A sudden drop-off in capacity is a symptom of battery failure, possibly a faulty cell. Gradual capacity reduction is expected. Some owners report charging faults where the charger and control unit stop communicating. Regular use and avoiding deep discharges are the best prevention. If the range drops significantly or charging fails, have the battery health checked.

problems.title: ZF 8HP Transmission Issues
problems.symptoms: Harsh or delayed shifting, jerky gear changes, torque converter shudder, transmission fluid leaks, failure to engage gear, "transmission jerky" complaints.
problems.repairCost: £500–£2,000 (fluid change, mechatronic repair, or solenoid replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if shifting issues develop

problems.recommendation: The ZF 8HP transmission is widely used across BMW's range and is generally robust, with a typical lifespan of 300,000–400,000 km when properly maintained. However, common issues include harsh or delayed shifting and mechatronic sleeve connector leaks. Some 530e owners have reported jerky gear changes, especially in 2nd gear. Many transmission failures trace back to fluid breakdown rather than catastrophic mechanical failure. BMW's "lifetime fill" claim is widely disputed — many specialists recommend transmission fluid changes every 60,000–80,000 miles.

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW 530e? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with oil changes at 8,000–10,000 mile intervals
* Cooling system inspected and no coolant loss history
* No oil leaks from the oil filter housing or valve cover
* Battery health report available showing capacity close to original
avoidIf: * No proof of regular oil changes — the B48 is unforgiving of neglect
* Coolant loss with no diagnosis — cooling system repairs can be complex
* Oil leaks present — oil filter housing or valve cover gaskets may be failing
* Significantly reduced electric range — battery degradation may be advanced
* Any engine warning light without diagnosis — the B48 and PHEV system can hide expensive faults

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The BMW 530e is the sweet spot in the 5 Series plug-in hybrid range — more power than the 520e, more efficient than the 545e, combining BMW's proven B48 four-cylinder engine with an electric motor and up to 12.0 kWh battery. It offers 252–292 PS, 420 Nm of torque, and up to 66 km of electric-only range. The B48 engine has proven largely reliable — timing chain problems are not a documented concern — but coolant system leaks and oil leaks from gaskets are the key maintenance items. The hybrid battery adds complexity and a potential long-term cost. Buy on service history, maintain on a shortened schedule, and this is one of the most accomplished executive PHEVs on the market.

cta.label: → Compare BMW 530e engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 530e (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Coolant Level — Check cold and hot; any loss indicates potential cooling system issues
* Service History — Oil changes at 8,000–10,000 mile intervals are essential for the B48
* Oil Leaks — Check for oil around the oil filter housing and valve cover
* Electric Range — Does the displayed range match the expected 28–41 miles? Significant drops indicate battery degradation
* Charging Test — Does it charge correctly? Any "unable to charge" messages?
* Transmission — Any harsh shifts or hesitation? ZF 8HP issues may be developing
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Cooling, oil, or PHEV system codes are most common
* MOT History — Check for emissions failures (often related to the complex emissions system)
